resources.string: Loading your local experience and choosing the right starting point.
A calm, local-first space for psychological self-reflection through the ten sephirot.
What feels most alive right now
Your most recent saved assessment was completed on %1$s, %2$s.
%1$s looked the most settled in your last reflection.
A gentle place to work from
Keep supporting %1$s through small, repeatable habits that help it stay steady.
Bring a little more steady attention to %1$s this week, without turning it into pressure.
Soften overcontrol around %1$s and look for a gentler rhythm this week.
Gathering your most recent saved assessment so this space can point to what matters now.
Once you complete an assessment, this space will show what felt most tense, what felt most settled, and a gentle focus for what to work with now.
Your latest reflection is not available right now
We could not load your latest saved assessment at the moment. You can still start a new reflection or open your saved results directly.
Review completed reflections, revisit earlier result overviews, and keep track of how your saved sessions have unfolded over time.
Read the deeper context behind the Tree of Life in psychologically grounded language.
Adjust language, appearance, and reflection preferences in a local-first settings space.

Numyah Mind helps you reflect on your current experience through the Tree of Life. It turns each sephira into grounded human themes such as boundaries, vitality, emotional flow, discernment, compassion, and daily action.\n\nYou are not here to be labeled. You are here to notice patterns, understand yourself with more nuance, and continue with greater clarity.

You will move through the ten sephirot one at a time: Malkhut, Yesod, Hod, Netzach, Tiferet, Gevurah, Chesed, Binah, Chokhmah, and Keter.\n\nBefore each section, you will get a short introduction in practical language. Then you will answer a small set of questions with a simple rating scale designed to feel calm and manageable on mobile.

Your results are described through three tendencies: balance, deficiency, and excess.\n\nBalance points to a more integrated expression. Deficiency suggests an area may be under-supported or harder to access right now. Excess suggests an area may be overextended or carrying more force than is helpful. These are current tendencies, not fixed identity statements.

When you finish, you will receive a full Tree of Life profile across all ten sephirot.\n\nYou will be able to open each area for a fuller interpretation, including healthy expression, deficiency patterns, excess patterns, and suggested practices. When a result is mixed, the language should stay soft and reflective instead of forcing a hard label.
Private, local, and ready for deeper learning

Your core experience is local to your device. Progress is saved privately, and the main assessment flow is designed to work offline.\n\nThis introduction keeps things simple. If you want deeper context later, you will be able to explore that in Learn and in each sephira result.
A calm introduction before you begin.
The Tree of Life, translated into lived experience
This assessment surface is still growing section by section as each sephira\'s questions and definitions are finalized.
Illustration for the current sephira assessment intro
A small note before you begin
This reflection works best with honesty

This reflection is most useful when your answers are honest about how things feel today.\n\nThere are no right answers here. You do not need to present your best self, only your real current experience.
What you will reflect on here
Your relationship with material life looks fairly grounded right now.
Your answers suggest Malkuth may be under-supported right now.
Your answers suggest Malkuth may be carrying too much force right now.
Your answers lean toward a grounded relationship with material life.
Your answers lean toward under-support in material life.
Your answers lean toward overextension in material life.
In practical terms, this points to a more stable connection with the body, possessions, and daily reality. You may be able to care for material life without becoming ruled by it.

In practical terms, this can mean the body, resources, or everyday structure are receiving less attention than they need. It does not mean failure. It suggests this area may need more care, steadiness, and presence.

In practical terms, this can mean money, possessions, control, or physical routines are taking up more space than is helpful. It suggests the material world may be carrying too much emotional weight right now.
This result is relatively balanced, but not strongly fixed. It suggests a generally grounded pattern with room for continued observation.
This result is not absolute. It suggests a current tendency to feel less rooted in the body, practical life, or material support.
This result is not absolute. It suggests a current tendency for material concerns or bodily control to take up more space than they need to.
Your answers formed a clear pattern in this section.
Your answers formed a noticeable pattern, with some mixed signals.
Your answers were more mixed here, so this result is best read as a gentle direction rather than a hard label.
This may show up as steady care for health, realistic management of money or possessions, and a sense that practical life supports you rather than pulls you away from yourself.
This may show up as neglecting the body, avoiding practical responsibilities, feeling disconnected from material stability, or treating basic needs as less important than everything else.
This may show up as overfocusing on control, productivity, possessions, image, or bodily discipline, to the point that material life starts to crowd out ease, flexibility, or emotional space.
Keep noticing what helps you stay grounded without becoming rigid. Small, repeatable habits are more useful here than pushing for perfection.
A gentle next step is to strengthen one basic act of care: food, rest, movement, money awareness, or tending to your environment. The goal is not intensity. It is steadiness.
A gentle next step is to loosen one area of overcontrol and ask what would feel supportive instead of demanding. The goal is not to abandon structure, but to make it more humane.
The assessment could not be loaded right now.
Your answer could not be saved. Please try again.
The assessment could not continue right now. Please try again.
The previous question could not be restored right now. Please try again.
Your progress is saved as you go. If you return home now, you can continue this reflection later.
This area will gradually grow into your full Tree of Life profile as more sephirot are completed and interpreted.
Finish the current reflection to see your saved Tree of Life overview here.
Results are not available right now
Your saved results could not be loaded right now. Please try again.
%1$d of %2$d sephirot have saved results in this completed assessment.
How the current tree is settling
The cards below are sorted from more unbalanced to more balanced, using deficiency plus excess as the main tension score while still showing all three forces.
%1$s is currently carrying the most tension, with an imbalance score of %2$d.
%1$s currently looks the most settled, with %2$d balance.
Loading the saved interpretation and score details for this part of your tree.
This sephira detail is not available
The saved score or content for this sephira could not be found in the selected results.
This detail could not be loaded
The interpretation for this sephira is not available right now. Please return to the results overview and try again.
%1$s is currently showing %2$s in this saved reflection.
Your saved assessments and future retakes will gather here as the history experience expands.
Gathering completed assessments from your device so you can revisit them calmly.
When you complete an assessment, it will appear here so you can revisit that reflection later.
History is not available right now
Your saved assessments could not be loaded right now. You can return to the assessment and try again later.
%1$d saved sessions are available from %2$s. Each one opens the full overview that was saved at that moment.
Each card shows when the reflection was completed and a quick sense of where the tree felt most tense and most settled.
%1$s carried the most tension in this reflection.
%1$s felt the most settled here, with %2$d balance.
This area will continue growing into the deeper educational companion to the assessment, with richer context drawn from the source material.
Explore longer-form reading built from the same Tree of Life source material, keeping much closer to its authored Kabbalistic teaching voice.
More courses will be added over time. The current catalog starts with the Tree of Life overview and is designed to grow gradually.
Loading the current course catalog from local content.
Courses are not available right now
The learning catalog could not be loaded right now. Please try again.
This course could not be found
The reading content for this destination is not available right now.
Finish the previous section first, then this one will open automatically.
%1$d of %2$d planned sections are ready to read in this course.
More sections will be added to this course over time. The current reading flow is designed to stay stable as new sephirot arrive.
Locked until the previous section is finished
Finish this section when you are ready. The next chapter opens only after the current one is marked complete.
This chapter is complete. You can continue forward, or return to an earlier section to reread it at your own pace.
Adjust how Numyah Mind feels on your device. This space covers language, appearance, assessment preferences, privacy guidance, and onboarding support.
Choose whether Numyah Mind follows your device language or stays in English or Spanish across the full app.
Language changes should apply across the full app so reading, assessment, and results stay aligned.
Use English across the app experience.
Use Spanish across the app experience.
Choose the appearance that feels most comfortable for reading and reflection across different environments.
Control a few experience details that shape how the reflection begins and feels on your device.
Keep the app bright and airy for daytime reading.
Use a softer dark palette for low-light reflection.
Show the short framing note before an assessment starts.
Return to the introduction whenever you want a fresh orientation to the framework and experience.
Start the welcome flow again without erasing your saved assessments.
This will open the introduction again and mark onboarding as incomplete, but it will not delete saved reflection data.
Review how your reflection data stays local to your device and what does or does not change when you replay onboarding.
Revisit the reflection-focused purpose of the app, the product boundary, and key context for reading your results.
How your reflection data is handled
Numyah Mind is designed to feel private, calm, and local-first. These notes explain what the current app stores on your device and what replaying onboarding does and does not change.
Your onboarding status, settings, in-progress assessment state, saved answers, and saved results are intended to stay on your device for the core v1 experience.
Used to support resume and history
Saved assessment data helps the app return you to an unfinished reflection and show your completed results later. It is there to support continuity, not to create a profile outside your device.
Replaying onboarding resets only the introduction flow so you can read the framing again. It does not erase saved assessments, answers, or results.
Numyah Mind is a self-reflection tool. It is not designed as a diagnostic, psychiatric, or clinical mental health product, and its saved data should be read in that same reflective spirit.
Local-first design is part of the trust model of this app, so privacy explanations should stay plain, calm, and easy to revisit.
A reflective tool, not a fixed label
Numyah Mind translates the Tree of Life into grounded psychological reflection. The app is meant to help you notice current tendencies, not to define who you are once and for all.
The goal is to offer a calm structure for self-observation through the ten sephirot, using language that is psychologically grounded, spiritually respectful, and non-pathologizing.

The Tree of Life is used here as a map for reflection. Each sephira becomes a practical area of experience that you can explore through short intros, questions, interpretations, and suggested practices.

Results describe present patterns such as balance, deficiency, or excess. They are intended as gentle directional language, especially when the signal is mixed, and should not be treated as a hard identity statement.
This app is still meant to feel human, practical, and reflective even when the language draws from a deeper symbolic tradition.
